# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template that creates a security group for EC2 instances. Allow SSH (port 22), HTTP (port 80), and HTTPS (port 443) access from anywhere. Our development team works remotely and needs flexible access.
# Category: cloud_network_security

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09' # Required at the top of all templates
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31  # Use serverless version for simplicity and more flexibility in template syntax. (optional)
Resources:                          # All resources are defined under Resources key, e.g., Templates or Mappings not allowed here!  
    Ec2SecurityGroup_SSHOnlyAccess:       # Unique logical ID of the resource – no spaces OR special characters supported; use lowercase alphanumeric and hyphen only for AWS Solution stack components (example : "asgf9h8347nqg" NOT ALLOWED HERE)
        Type: 'AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup'      # EC2 security group resource type – required.    The full definition in one-liner format would be provided below, or you can list the properties and their values as normal (for example : Properties/Value pairs).     e.g., {'Type': 'AWS:EC2::SecurityGroup', ...}
        Properties:                         # Define how to create a resource – required for each type at least once here   Example - {PropertyName}: PropertyValue, etc...     `PortRange(SSH):13456-9870 `    (this will add SSH rule allowing any inbound and outgoing traffic on port range defined.
            GroupId: 'sg'                      # The ID of the SecurityGroup to authorize egress for – requires if you provide security group ids list here or specify otherwise     EITHER use "Ref" key OR just declare an IPV4 Address with CIDR, example : {"CidrIp":...}/32
            SourceSecurityGroupName: 'YourEC2Instance' # The name of the Security Group to authorize ingress from – requires if you provide security group names here or specify otherwise     Either use "Ref" key OR declare a VPC Peering Connection ID, example : {'VpcPeeringConnectionId':...}
            Description: 'Allows SSH access only' # An optional description of the SecurityGroup. (optional) – required when modifying an existing security group to set/describe for audit purposes     EITHER use "Ref" key OR declare as a string   example : {'Description': ... }        Or you can specify all properties in normal format and provide list: `[ 'CidrIp',... ]`.
            Tags:                             # Optional metadata that is sent with AWS CloudTrail Logs (optional)     Example - {"Key": "string", “Value”:"String" }   OR declare as a string or use keys only, example : {'key1': 'value2'，...}`.
    Ec2SecurityGroup_AllHTTP:             # Unique logical ID of the resource (Example `ECRN9375JB0XMQG4AUE8IYT6RVFHKWZDNIUOJO1PNEIFCSSCSNN`)
        Type: 'AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup'      # EC2 security group resource type – required.    The full definition in one-liner format would be provided below, or you can list the properties and their values as normal (for example : Properties/Value pairs).     e.g., {'Type': 'AWS:EC2::SecurityGroup', ...}
        DependsOn: Ec2SecurityGroup_SSHOnlyAccess # Indicates that this resource should be created before "Ecrn9375JB0XMQG48UEIFY6RVFHCZDNIUOJO1PNEI" (if we have a reference here)
        Properties:                         # Define how to create the other security group – required for each type at least once.   Example - {PropertyName}: PropertyValue, etc...     `PortRange(HTTP):80-443 `    Here SSH port is being removed and replaced with HTTP ports from range (Example : 'http':'range', e.g., {'SourceSecurityGroupName: Ec2SecurityGroup_SSHOnlyAccess','FromPort': 1,'ToPort':9,...}
            GroupId: 'sg'                      # The ID of the Securitygroup to authorize ingress from - requires if you provide security group names here or specify otherwise     Either use "Ref" key OR declare a VPC Peering Connection id. example : {'VpcPeeringConnectionId': ... }  If using references, set DependsOn
            SourceSecurityGroupName: 'Ec2Instance' # The name of the Security Group to authorize ingress from - requires if you provide security group names here or specify otherwise     Either use "Ref" key OR declare a VPC Peering Connection id. example : {'VpcPeeringConnectionId': ...}
            Description:'Allows HTTP and HTTPS access' # An optional description of the SecurityGroup (optional) – required when modifying an existing security group to set/describe for audit purposes     Either use "Ref" key OR declare as a string   example : {'Description:... }    Or you can specify all properties in normal format and provide list - `['CidrIp', 'SourceSecurityGroupName'...]`.
            Tags:'Key':Value                  # Optional metadata that is sent with AWS CloudTrail Logs (optional)     Example {“key”:"string", ‘value’: "String" }   OR declare as a string or use keys only, example : {'tag1','Tag2'...}
